fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: North Dakota Physical Therapist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a physical therapist? Here's whether your license transfers to North Dakota - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: physical therapist license reciprocity north dakota, how to transfer physical therapist license to north dakota, physical therapist compact north dakota, north dakota physical therapist endorsement application

board.name: North Dakota Board of Physical Therapy
board.mailing_address:
North Dakota State Board of Physical Therapy
PO Box 450
Bismarck, ND 58502-0450

license_types.title: PT, DPT
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a physical therapist in North Dakota.

steps.title: Create an Account and Apply Online
steps.description: Go to the ND Licensing Portal, register for a new account, and then complete the 'Physical Therapist - Licensure by Endorsement' application, providing all required personal and professional information.

steps.title: Pay the Application Fee
steps.description: Submit the non-refundable application fee of $125.00 directly through the ND Licensing Portal as part of your online application submission.

steps.title: Submit Supporting Documentation
steps.description: Arrange for official transcripts to be sent directly from your PT program to the Board, and request official NPTE scores to be sent directly from FSBPT. If currently licensed, arrange for verification of your out-of-state license(s) to be sent directly to the Board.

steps.title: Complete the North Dakota Jurisprudence Exam
steps.description: Access and complete the online, open-book Jurisprudence Examination, based on North Dakota statutes and rules, via the ND Licensing Portal. This must be completed before a license is issued.

steps.title: Board Review and Licensure
steps.description: The Board will review your complete application, submitted documents, and exam results. Due to quarterly board meetings, processing time can vary. Upon approval, your North Dakota Physical Therapist license will be issued.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NPTE (National Physical Therapy Examination).

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the NPTE. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Physical Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

faqs.question: How much does a North Dakota physical therapist license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around $100 to $200. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the NPTE (National Physical Therapy Examination) is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does North Dakota have reciprocity for physical therapists?
faqs.answer: Yes. North Dakota participates in the PT Compact. If your home state and license qualify for compact privilege, you can typically practice in North Dakota without a separate endorsement application.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my physical therapist license to North Dakota?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks. If you qualify for an active compact privilege, there may be no separate wait to start practicing.

faqs.question: Can I start working in North Dakota while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Physical Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

hero.title: Become a Physical Therapist in North Dakota
hero.dek: To apply for a Physical Therapist license in North Dakota, applicants must use the North Dakota State Board of Physical Therapy's official online system, the ND Licensing Portal.

variants.label: New Applicant
variants.description: How to become licensed/certified for the first time

variants.label: Reciprocity
variants.description: How to transfer your existing credential from another state

variants.label: Renewal
variants.description: How to renew your existing credential

official_links.registration_steps: Applicants must register for a new account on the ND Licensing Portal by providing an email address and creating a password. Once registered, log in to select 'Start a New Application' and choose the appropriate license type (e.g., Physical Therapist - Licensure by Endorsement).
